verdict_reason: The engineering layer explicitly preserves the technical Sanskrit term samatvam in the body of the analog ('The verse names this state explicitly and gives it a name: samatvam, evenness, is what is being called yoga') and again in the concrete_scenario ('That non-movement is yoga in this verse's specific lexical sense — samatvam, evenness'). This is the gold-standard treatment for an Indian-philosophy-aware engineering analog: name the term, gloss it, do not flatten it. The yoga-sthaḥ phrase is treated as a state-condition ('Settle the operator first; act from the settled state') which is consistent across Shankara (bhūtvā samaḥ) and Ramanuja (established in yoga). Saṅgam is rendered as 'self-stake' / 'attachment' which is the universal-consensus reading; the Ramanuja-specific narrowing to kingdom/relatives is not invoked but also not contradicted. No tradition is conflated with another; no doctrinal-axis is smoothed. The falsifiability clause ('Equanimity is the absence of self-stake, not the absence of attention or care... has confused stoic suppression with samatvam') is precise — it specifically names what samatvam is NOT, which is exactly how the term should be guarded. Pass.
